policy: No public metric, ranking, or exported field may be introduced without a row here and a matching methodology note. Commercial users may view the public educational app, but downstream reuse must respect each source license.

rows.citation: Fick, S.E. and Hijmans, R.J. (2017), International Journal of Climatology.
rows.license: WorldClim public dataset terms; attribution required; downstream users must verify current terms before redistribution.
rows.method: Used as observed monthly baseline where available; CMIP6 anomaly layers are added on top.

rows.citation: NASA POWER Project, monthly meteorology point data derived from MERRA-2 and associated products.
rows.license: NASA open data; attribution requested. This registry uses reduced validation statistics, not bulk redistribution of source time series.
rows.spatialResolution: NASA POWER point service on underlying global reanalysis grid
rows.scenarioCoverage: observed/reanalysis climatology only; no future scenario coverage
rows.method: Used only for external observed-climatology validation of the packaged WorldClim baseline. Monthly PRECTOTCORR mm/day is converted to annual mm by month-length weighting. No forecast values are corrected from this source.

rows.version: AR6-era CMIP6 ensemble subset in packaged grid
rows.citation: Eyring et al. (2016), Geoscientific Model Development; ScenarioMIP and CMIP6 model outputs.
rows.license: Model-specific CMIP6 terms vary; registry permits display of reduced educational projections, not bulk redistribution of original model data.
rows.temporalResolution: decade anchors with interpolation in serving model
rows.scenarioCoverage: temperature/precipitation layers include SSP1-1.9, SSP1-2.6, SSP2-4.5, SSP3-7.0, SSP5-8.5; full app forecasts require ETCCDI coverage and currently serve SSP1-2.6/2-4.5/3-7.0/5-8.5
rows.method: Raw model-consensus anomaly is added to observed/model baseline; IPCC-calibrated temperature is shown alongside.

rows.provider: Journal of Applied Meteorology and Climatology / Roland Stull
rows.citation: Stull, R. (2011), Wet-Bulb Temperature from Relative Humidity and Air Temperature.
rows.license: American Meteorological Society publication terms apply; formula is implemented as cited method text, not redistributed article content.
rows.spatialResolution: inherits CMIP6 humidity and temperature input grid
rows.temporalResolution: monthly mean screen at interpolated scenario years
rows.scenarioCoverage: supported full-forecast app scenarios: SSP1-2.6, SSP2-4.5, SSP3-7.0, SSP5-8.5
rows.method: Empirical wet-bulb approximation from monthly mean air temperature and near-surface relative humidity. Used only as a humid-heat screening context; no daily exceedance days, WBGT, medical, or occupational-safety interpretation is inferred.

rows.citation: IPCC AR6 WGI Summary for Policymakers and assessed warming ranges.
rows.license: IPCC citation and reuse terms apply.
rows.method: Displayed beside raw CMIP6 temperature as assessed/calibrated context; not used to hide raw model output.

rows.citation: UNEP Emissions Gap Report 2025 current-policies and NDC warming projections.
rows.license: UNEP publication terms apply; registry uses the public report as cited policy context, not as redistributed source data.
rows.scenarioCoverage: policy and pledge cases, not a local SSP projection grid
rows.method: Used only to explain why SSP2-4.5 is the current-policy reference default; not used to adjust local grid values.

rows.citation: Climate Action Tracker 2025 global update on existing-policy warming projections.
rows.license: Climate Action Tracker website and publication terms apply; registry uses the public report as cited policy context, not as redistributed source data.
rows.scenarioCoverage: existing policies and target cases, not a local SSP projection grid
rows.method: Used only to explain why SSP2-4.5 is the current-policy reference default; not used to adjust local grid values.

rows.provider: IPCC AR6 / NASA Sea Level Projection Tool
rows.citation: IPCC AR6 sea-level projection data as distributed through NASA/JPL sea-level tools.
rows.license: NASA/IPCC attribution and dataset terms apply.
rows.method: Shown as coastal/regional context; inland local relevance is caveated.

rows.license: Natural Earth map data is public domain; attribution is requested but not required.
rows.spatialResolution: 1:110m generalized basemap line geometry; coarse global screening only
rows.scenarioCoverage: not scenario data; used only to caveat regional sea-level context by coarse coastal proximity
rows.method: Client computes nearest distance to the packaged Natural Earth 1:110m coastline line segments. The result is a coarse coastal-relevance flag only and does not include elevation, tides, surge, subsidence, defenses, rivers, drainage, or parcel exposure.

rows.citation: IPCC AR6 WGI Summary for Policymakers assessment of Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation change.
rows.license: IPCC citation and reuse terms apply.
rows.scenarioCoverage: qualitative AR6 assessment across emissions pathways
rows.method: Shown only as broad regional tail-risk context for relevant regions; not applied as a local deterministic correction.

rows.provider: Copernicus Climate Data Store / CMIP6 ETCCDI indicators
rows.citation: ETCCDI climate extreme indices derived from CMIP6 model output.
rows.license: Copernicus ETCCDI application states non-commercial research and education use; downstream commercial reuse is not approved by this registry.
rows.temporalResolution: decade anchors with interpolation in serving model
rows.scenarioCoverage: SSP1-2.6, SSP2-4.5, SSP3-7.0, SSP5-8.5; no SSP1-1.9 in this source
rows.method: Converted to transparent heat, drought, and flood pressure scores using documented thresholds.

rows.citation: fupit methodology, source-visible derived score from registered climate inputs.
rows.license: MIT code; source input licenses still govern downstream data reuse.
rows.temporalResolution: annual interpolated projections from registered inputs
rows.scenarioCoverage: supported full-forecast app scenarios: SSP1-2.6, SSP2-4.5, SSP3-7.0, SSP5-8.5
rows.method: Presentation score derived from temperature comfort, precipitation adequacy, heat stress, drought pressure, flood pressure, and adaptation placeholder documented in methodology.

rows.citation: Curated city/population-center examples used for communication, smoke tests, and bounded rankings.
rows.license: Project-maintained coordinates and labels; not a complete world city catalog.
rows.method: Catalog bounds hand-picked ranking candidates; rankings are educational examples and not global population-center claims.

rows.citation: Natural Earth populated places simple dataset, 1:110m cultural vectors.
rows.license: Natural Earth map data is public domain; attribution is requested but not required.
rows.scenarioCoverage: not scenario data; used only as a place catalog for precomputed grounded climate rankings
rows.method: Rows with pop_max >= 3,000,000 are used as a bounded population-place ranking catalog. Climate values still come from the grounded fupit grid, not from Natural Earth.

rows.provider: fupit derived catalog from Natural Earth
rows.version: natural-earth-vector 1:110m populated places simple, pop_max aggregate v1
rows.citation: Derived country aggregate from Natural Earth populated places simple dataset using pop_max weights.
rows.license: Derived from Natural Earth public-domain map data; attribution requested but not required.
rows.spatialResolution: country aggregate over included 1:110m populated-place points
rows.temporalResolution: static catalog weights; climate values inherit ranking artifact years
rows.scenarioCoverage: not scenario data; used only to aggregate precomputed grounded ranking metrics by scenario/year
rows.method: For each country represented by at least one included Natural Earth populated place, metric values are weighted across included points using the pop_max attribute.

rows.citation: Kuzma, S. et al. (2023), Aqueduct 4.0: Updated Decision-Relevant Global Water Risk Indicators. WRI Technical Note, doi:10.46830/writn.23.00061.
rows.licenseText: WRI datasets are available without restriction on use or distribution. WRI requests proper attribution.
rows.spatialResolution: HydroBASINS sub-basin polygons, rasterized to a 0.1 degree lookup grid
rows.scenarioCoverage: optimistic (SSP1-RCP2.6 -> ssp126), business-as-usual (SSP3-RCP7.0 -> ssp370), pessimistic (SSP5-RCP8.5 -> ssp585); ssp245 has no matching Aqueduct scenario and returns no value
rows.method: Water-stress category for the HydroBASINS sub-basin containing the queried point, looked up from a 0.1 degree rasterization of Aqueduct 4.0 future-annual polygons. A sub-basin prioritization screen, not a local or parcel-level water guarantee; covers water stress only, not drought, flood, quality, or sanitation access.

rows.provider: Quilcaille et al. 2023 (ETH Zurich), CMIP6 Canadian Fire Weather Index
rows.version: ETH Research Collection record (doi:10.3929/ethz-b-000583391), annual FWI indicators
rows.citation: Quilcaille, Y., Batibeniz, F., Ribeiro, A.F.S., Padron, R.S., Seneviratne, S.I. (2023), Showcasing global fire-weather projections in CMIP6 using a fully formulated Canadian Fire Weather Index. Earth System Science Data, doi:10.3929/ethz-b-000583391; code doi:10.5281/zenodo.7971275.
rows.licenseText: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC-BY 4.0). Verbatim from the ETH Research Collection record metadata: dc.rights.license = 'Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International', dc.rights.uri = http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/.
rows.scenarioCoverage: ssp126, ssp245, ssp370, ssp585 served directly (native SSP data, full coverage); ssp119 and over/434/460 experiments present in the archive but not served
rows.method: Multi-model ensemble mean (model-democracy) of the annual Canadian Fire Weather Index indicators extreme-fire-weather days and fire-season length, computed by Quilcaille et al. from daily mean relative humidity (hurs) and regridded to 2.5 degrees. Open-ocean cells are masked out. A coarse fire-conducive-weather screen, not actual fire risk, ignition, fuel, or land management.

rows.version: Aqueduct Floods v2 riverine inundation hazard maps (Ward et al. 2020)
rows.citation: Ward, P.J. et al. (2020), Aqueduct Floods methodology. WRI Technical Note; Aqueduct Floods hazard maps, World Resources Institute.
rows.licenseText: WRI datasets are available without restriction on use or distribution. WRI requests proper attribution (WRI Open Data Commitment, same terms cleared for the Aqueduct 4.0 water-stress artifact).
variables: mean riverine flood depth over flooded area
rows.spatialResolution: native ~1 km (30 arc-sec) inundation maps, block-reduced to a 0.1 degree lookup grid
rows.scenarioCoverage: RCP4.5 served as ssp245, RCP8.5 served as ssp585; ssp126 and ssp370 have no matching Aqueduct scenario and return no value
rows.method: 5-GCM ensemble-mean of the WRI Aqueduct Floods v2 riverine inundation-depth maps for the 1-in-100-year return period, block-reduced from ~1 km to a 0.1 degree grid storing the flooded-area fraction (depth > 5 cm) and the mean flood depth over flooded pixels. A regional flood-hazard screen dependent on assumed protection standards, not a property-level guarantee; riverine only, not coastal storm surge.
